title: Norfolk Southern marks major East Palestine milestone, completing intensive phase of environmental remediation
text: ATLANTA , Oct. 26, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Norfolk Southern Corporation (NYSE: NSC) announced Thursday soil excavation is set to be completed in East Palestine, Ohio, marking a significant environmental remediation milestone. This weekend, the final truckload of impacted soil will be removed from the derailment zone and transported offsite, which will substantially reduce heavy truck traffic on East Taggart Street and throughout the village.

title: Norfolk Southern launches AI train inspection technology
text: ATLANTA , Oct. 26, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Norfolk Southern Corporation (NYSE: NSC) is deploying Digital Train Inspection Portals to enhance rail safety across the company's 22-state network. The portals feature cutting-edge Machine Vision Inspection technology developed in partnership with the Georgia Tech Research Institution (GTRI), who engineered the hardware, and Norfolk Southern's Data Science/Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Mechanical teams, who built the brains behind the program.

title: Dividend Stock Watch List: Lanny's November 2023 Edition
text: Dividend investing happens, whether the stock market is up or down, whether the fed raises interest rates or lowers, inflation or deflation. UPS is projecting to earn between $85 billion to $100 billion in revenue, just large amounts, even during a tough year that we have all been through. NSC is down 25% this year, the stock is at/near the 52 week low, from well over $260 per share.

title: Norfolk Southern (NSC) Misses on Q3 Earnings, Decreases Y/Y
text: Low revenues from key segments hurt Norfolk Southern's (NSC) Q3 results.

title: Norfolk Southern (NSC) Reports Q3 Earnings: What Key Metrics Have to Say
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for Norfolk Southern (NSC) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended September 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: Norfolk Southern (NSC) Q3 Earnings Lag Estimates
text: Norfolk Southern (NSC) came out with quarterly earnings of $2.65 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.74 per share. This compares to earnings of $4.10 per share a year ago.

title: Norfolk Southern Q3 profit down sharply after Eastern Ohio derailment charge
text: Norfolk Southern Corp. NSC, -0.34% on Wednesday posted net income of $756 million, or $2.10 a share, for the third quarter, down from $1.3 billion a year ago. EPS was down 49% from a year ago, the company said in a statement.

title: Norfolk Southern reports third quarter 2023 results
text: ATLANTA , Oct. 25, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Norfolk Southern Corporation (NYSE: NSC) announced Wednesday its third quarter 2023 financial results. For the quarter, income from railway operations was $756 million and diluted earnings per share were $2.10.

title: Norfolk Southern Declares Quarterly Dividend
text: ATLANTA , Oct. 24, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Norfolk Southern Corporation (NYSE: NSC) announced Tuesday a quarterly dividend of $1.35 per share on its common stock. The dividend is payable November 20, 2023, to shareholders of record on November 3, 2023.

title: Is a Beat in Store for Norfolk Southern (NSC) in Q3 Earnings?
text: Norfolk Southern's (NSC) third-quarter 2023 revenues are expected to have been weighed down by segmental weakness.

title: UPS Gears Up to Release Q3 Earnings: What's in the Offing?
text: High labor and fuel costs are likely to have hurt UPS' third-quarter 2023 performance.

title: I'm Buying Norfolk Southern Stock With Both Hands
text: My strategy focuses on top-tier companies in sectors with strong long-term earnings power, pricing power, and low risks of disruption.

title: Norfolk Southern and Brotherhood of Railroad Signalmen partner to enhance safety
text: Pioneering initiative will launch innovative safety pilot program ATLANTA , Oct. 19, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Norfolk Southern Corporation (NYSE: NSC) and the Brotherhood of Railroad Signalmen (BRS) announced a partnership Thursday to create an innovative new program called the Signal Safety Collaboration. This one-year pilot program, which was developed with input from the Federal Railroad Administration (FRA), will establish a collaborative process – including joint inspections, information sharing, and training – to identify further improvements and best practices that enhance signal safety for the benefit of our employees and the communities we serve.

title: EPA orders Norfolk Southern to perform additional cleanup following Ohio derailment
text: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) on Wednesday ordered Norfolk Southern Corp to carry out additional cleanup in East Palestine, Ohio, following the Feb. 3 derailment of a company-operated freight train.

title: 2 REIT-Like Dividend Stocks We're Buying With Both Hands
text: I discuss two stocks that offer high income, resilience during recessions, capital appreciation, and a high barrier to market entry. Norfolk Southern is a wide-moat railroad giant with a history of dividend growth, offering attractive buying opportunities despite recent headwinds. CME Group, a financial derivatives powerhouse, offers consistent income through dividends and special dividends and is well-positioned to benefit from rising rates and market volatility.

title: The Pros Got It Wrong: 7 Stocks to Buy Despite Recent Downgrades
text: Analyst downgrades can be a valuable tool when making investment decisions. However, just because an analyst has changed his or her rating on a stock from “buy” to “hold,” from “buy” to “sell” or from “hold” to “sell,” doesn't mean you need to follow suit.

title: 2 Railroad Stocks With Solid Dividend Yield You May Count On
text: Here, we pick two railroad stocks, Union Pacific (UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NSC), with a solid five-year dividend growth history.

title: Boarding Norfolk Southern Corporation (Rating Upgrade)
text: Norfolk Southern Corporation shares are down 13.4% in the past 2 months, making them a more attractive investment. Traffic patterns show a decline in hauling of forest and building materials and energy, potentially indicating a slowdown in the broader economy. The stock is now cheaper than it was when I bought last April, and the dividend yield is much higher. The current valuation of Norfolk Southern is attractive.
